{{Infobox artifact
| name = Dragon's Fang
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Dragon's Fang Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Dragon's Fangs are specialized weapons forged by the figure known as the Wanderer, also called [[Valin]]. These blades serve as keys to the realm of [[Valinhall]] and were originally granted to the twelve founding members of the [[Dragon Army]]. While thirteen such weapons were created, several have been lost over time; currently, only four remain active. The Fangs are crafted from nearly indestructible [[Tartarus steel]] and possess the ability to cut through almost any substance, including stone, trees, and heavy armor. These weapons are unique to [[Valinhall Travelers]], who use them to summon power into themselves rather than summoning external forces. While a [[Traveler]] can call upon their [[Fang]] from the outside world, those within [[Valinhall]] cannot summon weapons from elsewhere. The Fangs exhibit various physical characteristics: some are long, slightly curved swords, while others may take the form of warhammers. They can vary in appearance, ranging from gleaming gold and silver to being chipped, pitted, or marked with black spots. Some Fangs are noted for having a level of independent thought. Notable examples include [[Mithra]], the thirteenth and final [[Fang]] forged by [[Valin]], and [[Seijan]], the tenth [[Fang]].}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The Dragon's Fangs are swords forged by the Wanderer and bestowed upon the twelve original soldiers of the [[Dragon Army]]. Although thirteen such weapons were originally created, only four remain active in the current era. These blades possess the unique ability to be summoned by a wielder who has earned their respect. During his trials, which are designed to test the physical strength required to wield such a weapon, [[Simon]] carries an undersized replica from the [[Valinhall armory]]. His proficiency is demonstrated when he slices through [[Erastes]]' bonds with an ease comparable to using a true Dragon's [[Fang]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Dragon's Fang]] is a [[sword]] forged by the Wanderer and given to each of the twelve original soldiers of the [[Dragon Army]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9">''House of Blades'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Only four of the thirteen swords (Dragon's Fangs) remain in use.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Only four of the thirteen Dragon's Fangs are currently active.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c10">''House of Blades'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Dragon's Fang]] is a weapon that can be summoned if its respect has been earned.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] carries an undersized replica of a [[Dragon's Fang]] from the [[Valinhall armory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c11">''House of Blades'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The trial is intended to ensure physical strength for one of the Dragon’s Fangs.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13">''House of Blades'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] sliced [[Erastes]]' bonds as easily as he could have with a Dragon’s [[Fang]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15">''House of Blades'', chapter 15</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Forged]] by the Wanderer from [[Tartarus steel]], Dragon's Fangs are gracefully curving swords that serve as lost artifacts. These weapons are used by various individuals, including [[Kai]], [[Indirial]], [[Denner]], [[Simon]], and the Wanderer, while [[Andra]] wears one buckled to her belt. A Dragon's [[Fang]] can assist in opening a Gate, facilitate easier travel to [[the House]], and is a weapon that only a chosen daughter can bear. When these swords clash, they produce an ear-shattering noise, and the thirteenth blade in existence has a designated heir.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The battle-axe [[Kai]] used is not a [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Dragon’s Fangs are gracefully curving swords forged by the Wanderer.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The Dragon’s Fangs are made of [[Tartarus steel]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Dragon's Fang]] is used to assist in opening a Gate.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Kai]] had recently had the Dragon’s [[Fang]] back.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Dragon's Fang]] is a [[sword]] used by [[Kai]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Denner]] holds a [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Kai]]'s [[Dragon's Fang]] is in the Wanderer's hands.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Dragon's Fang]] is a [[sword]] used by [[Indirial]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The clash of Dragon's Fangs creates an ear-shattering noise.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] and the Wanderer both possess Dragon's Fangs.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A [[Dragon's Fang]] is something a chosen daughter can bear.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A [[Dragon's Fang]] is required for easier traveling to [[the House]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The thirteenth [[Dragon's Fang]] has an heir.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The Dragon’s [[Fang]] is a weapon wielded by [[Denner]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Dragon’s Fangs are lost artifacts.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Andra]] wears a Dragon’s [[Fang]] buckled onto a belt.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Dragon’s [[Fang]] is a weapon.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A Dragon's [[Fang]] is a classification of [[Mithra]] weapon used by [[Valinhall Travelers]], consisting of thirteen [[Valinhall]]-forged swords that feature a smooth vein of gold running down the flat of the blade. These sentient blades are capable of cutting through stone walls, chains, and trees, and they possess the power to penetrate an [[Incarnation]]'s rib cage or push through polished armor. While [[Simon]] once found the weapon too heavy to lift, he eventually used his own Dragon's [[Fang]] to strike [[Asphodel]]'s neck. Other notable examples include [[Andra]]'s ink-spotted blade, [[Valin]] the Wanderer's gold-and-silver weapon, and the mirror-bright [[sword]] held by [[Erastes]]. Throughout these events, various individuals such as [[Indirial]], [[Kai]], and [[Kathrin]] possess or interact with these artifacts, some of which were even taken hostage.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Dragon's Fang]] is a type of weapon or classification for [[Mithra]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5">''City of Light'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ilana]] avoided the point of the Dragon’s [[Fang]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall Travelers]] use [[Dragon's Fang]] swords.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13">''City of Light'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Dragon's Fangs are [[Valinhall]]-forged swords capable of cutting through trees, chains, and stone walls.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Dragon's Fang]] can split [[Alin]]'s armor and penetrate an [[Incarnation]]'s rib cage.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]]' [[sword]] shines mirror-bright like one of the Dragon's Fangs.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Andra]]'s [[Dragon's Fang]] has a blade spattered with black spots like ink.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Dragon's Fang]] is a [[sword]] with a smooth vein of gold running down the flat of the blade.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Dragon's Fang]] is a blade that can push through polished armor.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|There are thirteen Dragon's Fangs.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16">''City of Light'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Six Dragon's Fangs are currently known to [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] possesses one [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] possesses one [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Andra]] possesses one [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Kai]] possesses one [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Dragon's Fang]] is an object [[Simon]] was too weak to lift.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] uses the Dragon’s [[Fang]] to strike [[Asphodel]]'s neck.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] raises his Dragon’s [[Fang]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] intends to return the Dragon’s Fangs.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The two [[Incarnations]] tossed [[Indirial]] three Dragon’s Fangs.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Denner]]'s [[Dragon's Fang]] has been removed.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The edge of the [[Dragon's Fang]] scrapes at walls and doorframes.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ornheim]] gathered three Dragon’s Fangs.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Dragon’s [[Fang]] of [[Valin]], the Wanderer, is a gold-and-silver weapon.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]]'s grip closes around his [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] uses the Dragon’s [[Fang]] in his left hand.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23">''City of Light'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Dragon's Fangs are capable of thinking for themselves.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Dragon's Fangs were taken hostage.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Dragon's Fang]] is an artifact held by [[Kathrin]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"They are keys to the House, forged by the Wanderer himself and presented to the twelve original soldiers of the Dragon Army."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"Only four of the thirteen swords remain in use."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|"In the Wanderer's hands," Kai responded.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|"I know your daughter was chosen to bear a Dragon's Fang—you are to be congratulated."<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“The Dragon’s Fangs will be returned, along with all that we’re missing.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Artifacts]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate artifacts]]
